symbols: arrows piercing the body — the instruments of martyrdom and the price of faithfulness to Christ under persecution
exposed wounded torso — the vulnerability of the human body offered in sacrifice for faith
attendants removing arrows — compassionate care for the suffering saint, echoing the community of believers who minister to the persecuted
dark background — the darkness of persecution and the hiddenness of faith under imperial Rome
description: A dark, dramatically lit painting depicting a young man with a bare, pale torso reclining on the ground, his head thrown back and mouth slightly open in an expression of pain. He wears only a white loincloth. Two or three dark-clothed figures lean over him from behind, apparently tending to him or removing arrows from his body. The lighting is stark and focused on the pale flesh of the central figure, with the surrounding figures and background plunged into near-total darkness. The composition emphasizes the contrast between the luminous, suffering body and the enveloping shadows.
